text:
Union Arena (ユニオンアリーナ, Yunion Arīna) is a trading card game launched by Bandai. Bandai has collaborated with Hunter × Hunter, as well as many other titles.

Overview

Hunter × Hunter was one of the earliest titles to get a Union Arena release, launching during the first wave in March 2023 along with four other titles. In the English launch, Hunter × Hunter was one of the first three titles to get a release announced. The Japanese Hunter × Hunter Union Arena series currently has two booster pack volumes, while the English version has only has one with the second set for release on September 26, 2025.

Hunt your opponent with Comrades × Courage × Wisdom! (仲間×勇気×知恵で相手を狩れ!) was the slogan used on the releases of the main Japanese products and later the English products as well, but only on the official pages for the products, and not on any of the packaging. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/en/products/boosters/htr-1.php https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/decks/htr-1.php https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/boosters/htr-1.php 

The main collection uses 5 rarity types for its cards: Common (C), Uncommon (U & U★), Rare (R & R★), Super Rare (SR & SR★ & SR★★ & SR★★★), and Action Point (AP). All of the ★ rarities are technically considered Parallel Rare, as they all have a parallel foil on them. Some of the promotional event cards have additional rarities, such as Union Rare (UR), OBC, and PR.

Many of the cards in the collection utilize art from the 2011 anime adaptation; however, some of the cards use art exclusive to the Union Arena collection.

Ver.0

Promotion Pack Ver.0 (プロモーションパック Ver.0) was the first available Union Arena pack, and was given out as a promotional item to those who visited the Bandai booth at Jump Festa 2023, from December 17 th , 2022 to December 18 th , 2022. The pack contained three cards, and was given out in a Union Arena shopping bag. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/jumpfesta/2023/ The packaging of the card pack features Gon, as does the shopping bag and one of the three cards in the pack. https://x.com/UNION_ARENA_TCG/status/1604266432107532288 

The Gon card from the pack would also be given out as a prize at future events, such as the UA Teaching Tours, https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/teaching-tour/ Gunslinger Battle Uniari Festival 2023, and Gunslinger Battle - BCG Fest.

 
Union Arena Promotion Pack Ver.0.png|Ver. 0 Pack
Union Arena UAPR Card 076 U.png|Gon Card
Union Arena Jump Festa '23 Promotion Pack Ver.0 & Shopping Bag Promo.png|Promo featuring the Union Arena shopping bag
 

Premium Card Set

The Union Arena Premium Card Set (ユニオンアリーナ プレミアムカードセット), part of the UA03PB series, was the first available card set for Union Arena's exclusive Hunter × Hunter line, available for reservation during the period of February 19 th 2023 to February 28 th 2023 in Japan. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/products/other/p-bandai/htr-cardset.php The Premium Card Set has not had an English release.

The set contained four cards in total (1 Character card and 3 Action Point cards), all of which are exclusive to the set. The cards came packaged in a folder and were originally sold for 1,000 yen.

Demo Deck

The English release of Union Arena launched an early Trial Event before the actual official release of any Union Arena cards in English. The event took place at Bandai Card Games Fest 23 -24 in Los Angeles, from January 27 th , 2024 to January 28 th , 2024. To participate in the event, players could choose from and receive one of three Demo Decks available (corresponding to each of the three titles releasing their Union Arena collabs first, including Hunter × Hunter). https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/bcgfest/23-24/trial/ 

The Demo Deck (UE02 DEMO) was also given to players who participated in the following events:
Union Arena local store events in mid-March of 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/demo_deck_event_at_localstores/2024/ 
LVL Up EXPO from April 26 – 28, 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/lvl_up_expo/ 
C2E2 2024 from April 26 – 28, 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/c2e2/2024/ 
Trial session held at Gen Con 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/gencon/2024/ 
Trial Event at Anime NYC 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/anime_nyc/2024/ 
Trial session held at New York Comic Con 2024 https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/nycc/2024/ 
Trial session held at Anime Expo 2025 in Los Angeles https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/anime_expo/2024/ 
Trial session at the Shonen Jump pop-up store in New York City https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/shonen-jump-store-pop-up/ and California. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/shonen-jump-store-pop-up-california/ 

The majority of the cards in the Hunter × Hunter Demo Deck have the same card art as cards that had already been released in various Union Arena sets in the Japanese version of the game. However, the Demo Deck also includes three cards with unique card art not yet present in any of the main Union Arena Japanese sets (UA03BT, UA03ST, or EX01BT). https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/news/006.php 

 
Union Arena UE02 Card 010 U.png|Demo Deck unique card art - Killua
Union Arena UE02 Card 001 R.png|Demo Deck unique card art - Kurapika
Union Arena UE02 Card 004 R.png|Demo Deck unique card art - Gon
 

Starter Deck

The Japanese Starter Deck (UA03ST) was released on March 24 th , 2023, alongside Booster Pack Vol. 1, and is designed for beginners. It includes a 50-card deck, 3 Action Point cards, and a playsheet. It contains some exclusive cards not available in the Booster Packs. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/products/decks/htr-1.php The standard Action Point cards that come with it are of Hunter Licences; however, there is a chance to receive a special Action Point card featuring Gon, Killua, Kurapika, and Leorio as well.

The English Starter Deck (UE02ST) released before the English version of Booster Pack Vol.1 on September 27 th , 2024. Like the Japanese version, it contains 50 cards, 3 Action Point cards, and one playsheet. The deck also has the same chance as the Japanese version of receiving a special shiny Action Point card with alternate art featuring Gon, Killua, Kurapika, and Leorio, instead of the standard Action Point card. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/decks/htr-1.php 

 
Union Arena UA03ST Starter Deck.png|Japanese Starter Deck (UA03ST)
Union Arena UE02ST Starter Deck.png|English Starter Deck (UE02ST)
 

Booster Pack Vol. 1

 
REPLACE.png |English booster pack (UE02BT)
Union Arena UA03BT Booster Pack.png |Japanese booster pack (UA03BT)
 
The individual Japanese Booster Packs (UA03BT) contain eight cards per pack, and can also be purchased as a Booster Box containing 20 Booster Packs (160 cards total). The North American version of Booster Pack Vol. 1 (UE02BT) was released on October 4, 2024, and contains 12 cards in each pack. In addition, the English release announced that Booster Packs have the chance to come with a special "signature version" card of Gon, Killua, Hisoka, Chrollo, or Illumi. The cards are the following:
Gon SR★★ foil card signed by Gon's 2011 Japanese voice actor, Megumi Han, including a dialogue line from Gon
Killua SR★★ foil card signed by Killua's 2011 Japanese voice actor, Mariya Ise, including a dialogue line from Killua
Hisoka SR★★ foil card signed by Hisoka's 2011 Japanese voice actor, Daisuke Namikawa, including a dialogue line from Hisoka
Chrollo SR★★ foil card signed by Chrollo's 2011 Japanese voice actor, Mamoru Miyano, including a dialogue line from Chrollo
Illumi SR★★ foil card signed by Illumi's 2011 Japanese voice actor, Masaya Matsukaze, including a dialogue line from Illumi

The UE02BT booster packs were available for purchase ahead of the official release date at participating North American Release Events running from September 27, 2024, until October 3, 2024. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/release_event/2024/ue02bt/ In addition, the release events also awarded participants with a special Release Event Pack containing 2 of the possible 73 cards, all of which were Common and Uncommon cards from the UE02BT set with a special "Release Event" stamp.

 
Union Arena UE02BT Card 078 SR++.png |Signed Gon SR★★ card
Union Arena UE02BT Card 094 SR++.png |Signed Killua SR★★ card
Union Arena UE02BT Card 043 SR++.png |Signed Hisoka SR★★ card
Union Arena UE02BT Card 037 SR++.png |Signed Chrollo SR★★ card
Union Arena UE02BT Card 050 SR++.png |Signed Illumi SR★★ card
Union Arena Release Event Pack.png|English Release Event Pack
Union Arena UEPR Card 099 U Release Event Ver..png|Example of a Release Event Pack card with Release Event stamp
 

Booster Pack Vol. 2

Booster Pack Vol. 2 (EX01BT) was first released in Japan on October 27 th , 2023. The volume as a whole contains 8 SR cards, 4 SR★ cards, 4 SR★★ cards, 1 SR★★★ card, 12 R cards, 6 R★ cards, 25 U cards, 3 U★ cards, 28 C cards, 1 C★ card, and 4 AP cards, for a total of 96 cards. Individual Booster Packs contain eight cards in total. Like Vol. 1, some of the cards in this release contain pack-exclusive illustrations.

The English version of Booster Pack Vol. 2 (UEX04BT) is set to release on September 26, 2025, with the release event period planned to be from September 19 to October 2, 2025. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/boosters/ex_htr.php Like with Booster Pack Vol.1 (UE02BT), Vol. 2 will have two special autographed cards to celebrate the release. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/news/028.php 

 
Union Arena EX01BT Booster Pack.png |Japanese Booster Pack Vol. 2 (EX01BT)
Booster Pack Vol. 2 UEX04BT Release Announcement Promo.png |Booster Pack Vol. 2 (UEX04BT) Release Promo
 

Official Sleeves Vol. 2

The official Vol. 2 card sleeves (オフィシャルカードスリーブ HUNTER×HUNTER Vol. 2) had their Japanese release on March 15, 2025, and come in a pack of 60. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/products/other/sleeve/htr-2.php They have also been available for purchase at a number of events since their release. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/uniare-fes/2025/tokyo/merchandise/ https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/uniare-fes/2025/aichi/merchandise/ https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/uniare-fes/2025/fukuoka/merchandise/ https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/uniare-fes/2025/hyogo/merchandise/ https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/uniare-fes/2025/revenge-match/merchandise/ 

They have not had an official North American release yet, but were available for purchase at the BANDAI CARD GAMES Fest 24-25 World Tour in Dallas. https://www.bandaicardgames-fest.com/24-25/en/dallas/products/02_29.html 

Playmat & Half Storage Box Set

The Playmat & Half Storage Box set officially released in North America on September 27, 2024, and contains one playmat and one storage box, both featuring Leorio, Kurapika, Gon, Killua, and Hisoka. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/other/playmat_storage_set/htr-1.php Months before its official release, the set could be pre-ordered and then retrieved at either Anime Expo 2024 or GenCon 2024. https://web.archive.org/web/20250426080427/https://p-bandai.com/us/cont/campaign/axgencon2024 If purchased this way at Anime Expo 2024, purchasers would also receive a special Action Point card, which can be found in the individual promotional cards table. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/anime_expo/2024/ The product has not had an official Japanese release.

Bandai Card Games Fest 23-24 Special Set

The Hunter × Hunter Bandai Card Games Fest 23 24 Special Set (BANDAI CARD GAMES Fest 23-24スペシャルセット) was first released in Japan on March 2 nd , 2024, for the Bandai Card Game Fest 23-24. The set includes a rubber playmat, a set of sixty card sleeves, three Action Point cards, and a storage box. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/products/other/bcgfest23-24_spset/htr.php 

 
Union Arena BCG Fest Special Set.png|BCG Fest 23-24 Special Set
Union Arena UAPB Card 04 AP Bandai Card Games Fest 23-24.png|Action Point card from the set
 

Promotional Cards

Jump Festa 2023 Promotion Pack Ver.0

The Promotion Pack Ver. 0 (プロモーションパック Ver. 0) was available on December 17 th , 2022, and December 18 th , 2022, to those who visited the Bandai booth at Jump Festa 2023. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/events/jumpfesta/2023/ The folder contained three Union Arena UAPR cards, one of which features Gon.

The Gon card on its own has been distributed at other Union Arena events besides Jump Festa 2023. For the specific events it has been available at, see its listing in the individual promotional cards table.

Action Point Card Pack / Action Point Vol. 1

The Action Point Card Pack Vol. 1 (アクションポイントカードパック VOL.1), part of the UAPR series, first became available April 29 th , 2023. The volume contains 5 exclusive Action Point cards, one from each of the titles in the collection, with each pack containing 1 card. The titles included in the collection are Code Geass, Jujutsu Kaisen, Hunter × Hunter, THE iDOLM@STER ShinyColors , and Demon Slayer. The Hunter × Hunter AP card (UAPR 01) features Chrollo, Kurapika, Killua, Gon, and Leorio.

The English version of the pack is simply called Action Point Card Pack, and contains the English version of the same Hunter × Hunter card (UEPR 01) as the Japanese pack. The pack varies in other ways, however, as there are eight total cards in the English pack (4 normal and four silver-foiled), and the three other titles included in the pack are Code Geass, Jujutsu Kaisen, and Bleach. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/store-tournament/2024/ 

The packs have and continue to be distributed as part of prize pools for many different Union Arena events. A sleeve set of three bearing the Hunter × Hunter Action Point card design has also been used for event prizes. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/rare-battle/2024/12/ https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/rare-battle/2025/large-scale/ 

 
Union Arena Action Point Pack Vol 1 Packaging.png |Action Point Vol. 1 (Japanese)
Union Arena UAPR Card 01 AP.png |Action Point Vol. 1 Hunter × Hunter UAPR card
Union Arena Action Point Card Pack.png|Action Point Card Pack (English)
Union Arena UEPR Card 01 AP.png|Action Point Card Pack Hunter × Hunter UEPR card
Union Arena HxH Action Point Card Sleeve set prize.png|Action Point Sleeve Design
 

Action Point Vol. 3

The Action Point Card Pack Vol. 3 (アクションポイントカードパック VOL.3), part of the UAPR series, first became available August 26 th , 2023. The volume contains six exclusive Action Point cards, one from each of the titles in the collection, with each pack containing 1 card.

The Hunter × Hunter AP card features the Phantom Troupe.

The pack has and continues to be distributed as part of prize pools for many different Union Arena events.

 
Union Arena Action Point Vol. 3 Pack.png |Action Point Vol. 3
Union Arena UAPR Card AP02.png |Action Point Vol. 3 Phantom Troupe UAPR card
 

Judge Pack

The Judge Pack is part of the UEPR series and is a special pack given as compensation for judges of specific English Union Arena events, first at the Store Tournaments held from October 1, 2024, until December 31, 2024. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/store-tournament/2024/ The pack contains 1 card out of a possible 10, and includes several other titles alongside Hunter × Hunter.

The two possible Hunter × Hunter cards from the pack are special judge versions of Gon and Kurapika cards from Booster Pack Vol. 1.

 
Union Arena Judge Pack.png|Judge Pack
Union Arena UEPR Card 005 C Judge Ver..png|Judge Pack Gon UEPR Card
Union Arena UEPR Card 071 C Judge Ver..png|Judge Pack Kurapika UEPR Card
 

Event Pack Vol. 1

Event Pack Vol. 1, part of the UEPR series, first became available as a prize at the UNION ARENA RARE BATTLE event, which ran from December 6, 2024, to January 12, 2025. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/rare-battle/2024/12/ It will likely continue to be a prize at future events.

The volume contains eight cards, two from each of the titles in the collection, with each pack containing 1 card. The titles included in the collection are Code Geass, Jujutsu Kaisen, Hunter × Hunter, and Bleach. The two cards from each franchise have the same number and image as each other, and are only differentiated by one being gold-foiled.

The Hunter × Hunter cards (both UEPR 077) feature Gon.

 
Union Arena Event Pack Vol.1.png|Event Pack Vol.1
Union Arena UEPR Card 077 R.png|Gon UEPR Card
Union Arena UEPR Card 077 R Gold Foil.png|Gon UEPR Card (Gold Foil)
 

Super Pre-release Starter Deck & Event

The North American release released an exclusive pre-launch Starter Deck (UE02ST PRE) from August 23, 2024, to September 8, 2024, titled the Super Pre-Release Starter Deck. The Pre-Release 50-card deck contains the same cards as the regular Starter Deck; however, the cards from the Super Pre-Release Starter Deck have a special gold stamp on them. The deck also came with 3 Action Point cards and three index cards. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/events/super_pre-release_event/2024/ 

The Super Pre-Release Starter Deck launched alongside an event, called the Super Pre-Release Event, where participants were required to purchase a Super Pre-Release Starter Deck to participate. Participants and winners of the event would receive corresponding Kurapika UEPR cards, which can be found in the Miscellaneous Promotional Cards section.

Miscellaneous Promotional Cards

Some promotional cards distributed as prizes for participating in Union Arena events do not belong to specific volumes or sets, but are single UAPR, UEPR, or URPR cards.

The following is a list of the single UAPR, UEPR, and URPR cards and the events at which they were distributed:

Union Arena Month - DECKS STADIUM

From March 15 th , 2023 to May 25 th , 2023, the Bandai Namco official card game shop DECKS STADIUM held Union Arena Month. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/news/008.php During this period, the shop was themed around the prominent Union Arena titles of the time, including Hunter × Hunter.

 
Union Arena Month Game Center Display 1.png|In-store image 1
Union Arena Month Game Center Display 2.png|In-store image 2
 

Union Arena Tutorial App

The Union Arena Tutorial App first launched in Japanese on October 25 th , 2023, and was designed to help players learn the basics of the game. It launched with both an Android and iOS version of the app, as well as a browser-based version.

The Japanese version launched alongside an event that ran from October 25 th , 2023, until November 14 th , where players could play online in a campaign. A lottery prize of a set of three of the Union Arena Starter Decks, including the Hunter × Hunter Starter Deck (UA03ST), was available for this event. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/news/051.php 

The English version of the browser app launched on December 28 th , 2023, alongside the official English release of Union Arena. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/news/001.php 

 
Union Arena Tutorial App Promotional Launch.png |Union Arena Tutorial App Japanese Launch Promotion
Union Arena TCG Tutorial App Browser Ver 1.0.0.png |Union Arena Tutorial App English Browser Version

Trivia

Promotion Pack Ver.0 often uses Ver.0 on its packaging and in official articles, although some official promo for it also refers to it as Vol.0.
The Japanese Booster Pack Vol.2 (EX01BT) had originally been titled UNION ARENA Extra Booster Pack HUNTER×HUNTER (UNION ARENA エクストラブースターパック HUNTER×HUNTER), before later being changed to UNION ARENA Booster Pack HUNTER×HUNTER Vol.2 (UNION ARENA ブースターパック HUNTER×HUNTER Vol.2), https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/jp/news/040.php which is why the product number for the pack (EX01BT) and the printing on the cards contain the "EX" (for "extra").
The official site refers to the English Starter Deck (UE02ST) as a "Greed Island Arc themed deck," however, the cards exclusive to the Starter Deck are not of Greed Island characters. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/products/decks/htr-1.php 
 
Union Arena UEPR Card 026 U Release Event Ver..png|Patch of shore (correct version)
Union Arena UE02BT Card 026 U.png|Strip of Beach
Union Arena UEPR Card 063 C Release Event Ver..png|Incredibly fast chop (correct version)
Union Arena UE02BT Card 063 C.png|Scarily Fast Chop
 
The English Booster Pack Vol.1 (UE02BT) contains errors in the titles of two cards, which were not present in the titles of the Release Event versions of the same cards. https://www.unionarena-tcg.com/na/news/014.php These cards are:
Patch of shore, which is incorrectly titled "Strip of Beach" in Booster Pack Vol.1
Incredibly fast chop which is incorrectly titled "Scarily Fast Chop" in Booster Pack Vol.1
The names of these cards should be treated as their Release Event names, not their Booster Pack Vol.1 names.
